project: Influx v1 — PRD 09: RSS Fetcher + Multi-Profile Orchestration + Backfill
description: Extends the arXiv-only single-profile pipeline along three independent axes: (S-11) an RSS/Atom fetcher in `src/influx/sources/rss.py` using `feedparser`, with per-feed `source_tag` (`rss` or `blog`) flowing into the canonical `source:*` tag and the archive bucket layout `/archive/<feed.source_tag>/{YYYY}/{MM}/{feed-slug}-{YYYY-MM-DD}-{url-hash}.html` where `{url-hash}` is the first 10 hex chars of SHA-256(normalised `source_url`); (S-12) multi-profile orchestration with `profile:*` tag union-merging on shared notes, per-profile `## Profile Relevance` bullet merging by profile name, per-profile rejection authority preserved on the ingest path (`influx:rejected:<profile>` blocks re-adding `profile:<profile>` even when the source matches that profile again), cross-profile parallelism with same-profile strict serial (already enforced by PRD 03's coordinator), and per-source fetch deduplication across profiles within a single scheduled fire (R-8); (S-13) a real `POST /backfills` handler in `src/influx/http_api.py` replacing PRD 03's stub estimator, with naive cost estimate `days × len(categories) × max_results_per_category` (Q-3), a `--confirm` gate triggered by `estimated_items > 1000` (FR-BF-6), `lithos_task_create(tags=["influx:backfill", ...])` task tagging, no webhook POST (FR-NOT-4), and no repair sweep (FR-REP-2) — both gates already exist from PRDs 05/06 but this PRD ensures `kind="backfill"` is correctly propagated from the API down. After this PRD, the v1 product is functionally complete; PRD 10 adds OTEL spans/metrics and rejection-rate logging.

userStories.title: Add feedparser dependency and RSS source parser with recorded fixtures
userStories.description: As an Influx operator, I want an RSS/Atom parser module so that feeds configured with `source_tag` of `rss` or `blog` produce per-item records (title, URL, published, summary) for downstream ingest, with each item inheriting its feed's `source_tag` verbatim (FR-SRC-4).
acceptanceCriteria: Add `feedparser` to project dependencies (e.g. `pyproject.toml`)

Create `src/influx/sources/rss.py` exposing a `feedparser`-based RSS/Atom parser that, given feed content and the feed's configured `name`, `url`, and `source_tag`, returns per-item records with at minimum `title`, `url`, `published`, `summary`
Each parsed item carries the feed's configured `source_tag` verbatim (FR-SRC-4); the parser does not infer or override `source_tag`
Recorded feed fixtures live under `tests/fixtures/rss/*.xml` covering at least one Atom feed and one RSS 2.0 feed

Unit test `tests/unit/test_rss_fetcher.py` covers: (1) Atom parsing yields the documented fields, (2) RSS 2.0 parsing yields the documented fields, (3) a feed configured with `source_tag="rss"` yields items tagged `rss`, (4) a separate feed configured with `source_tag="blog"` yields items tagged `blog`
No feed contributes items to more than one `source:*` bucket

userStories.title: URL-hash for archive filename disambiguation with collision and determinism tests
userStories.description: As the archive writer, I want a deterministic `url-hash` so that two distinct items from the same feed published on the same date map to distinct archive filenames, and re-ingesting the same URL always yields the same filename (AC-M3-4 sub-tests 2 and 3).
acceptanceCriteria: Compute the first 10 hex chars of SHA-256 over the normalised `source_url` for use as the archive `{url-hash}` segment
The hash is computed over the canonical normalised `source_url` so it is stable across call sites
Determinism unit test in `tests/unit/test_archive_url_hash.py`: hashing the same URL twice yields the same 10-char hex string (AC-M3-4 sub-test 3 / AC-09-C)
Collision unit test: two URLs differing only by path segment (e.g. `https://feed-x.example/post-a` and `https://feed-x.example/post-b`) yield two distinct hash suffixes (AC-M3-4 sub-test 2 / AC-09-B)
The hash is derived from `normalise_url(source_url)` so callers operating on equivalent URLs receive the same hash

userStories.title: RSS archive layout and note-path integration end-to-end
userStories.description: As the Influx ingest pipeline, I want RSS items routed to source-tag-specific archive and note paths so that `source_tag`, the `source:*` tag, the note storage path, and the archive path all agree, satisfying AC-M3-4 sub-test 1.
acceptanceCriteria: RSS items are archived at `/archive/<source_tag>/{YYYY}/{MM}/{feed-slug}-{YYYY-MM-DD}-{url-hash}.html`, where `{url-hash}` is the first 10 hex chars of SHA-256 over the normalised `source_url`
RSS article fetches go through PRD 02's guarded HTTP client so that the SSRF guard and size/timeout caps apply (FR-SRC-5 / FR-RES-4)
Notes for `source_tag="rss"` are stored at `articles/rss/{YYYY}/{MM}` and tagged `source:rss`
Notes for `source_tag="blog"` are stored at `articles/blog/{YYYY}/{MM}` and tagged `source:blog`
The archive `{source}` segment, the note's `source:*` tag, and the note's storage path (FR-NOTE-2) all agree (AC-M3-4 sub-test 1 / AC-09-A)

Integration test `tests/integration/test_rss_to_lithos.py` drives an end-to-end ingest of a fixture feed and asserts the archive file exists on disk and the resulting note carries the expected `source:*` tag and storage path

End-to-end collision test (AC-09-B): ingesting two items from the same feed published on `2026-04-23` with URLs `https://feed-x.example/post-a` and `https://feed-x.example/post-b` produces two distinct archive files; both exist on disk after the run; neither overwrites the other

Determinism path-computation test (AC-09-C): hashing the same normalised URL `https://feed-x.example/post-a` twice yields the same `{url-hash}` and therefore the same archive filename component, verified at the helper / path-builder level without requiring a second end-to-end ingest

userStories.title: RSS web extraction fallback to feed summary when body is below min_web_chars
userStories.description: As the article enrichment step, I want to fall back to the feed `<summary>` when the extracted web article body is shorter than `extraction.min_web_chars` so that thin or paywalled RSS items still produce usable notes (AC-09-J, FR-ENR-3).
acceptanceCriteria: When PRD 07's web extractor rejects an article because the extracted body fails the `extraction.min_web_chars` minimum (e.g. raises `ExtractionError`), the ingest path falls back to using the feed item's `<summary>` instead of the extracted body (FR-ENR-3)

Integration test covers the extractor-rejection / min-length-failure path with a fixture whose article body is intentionally short and asserts end-to-end that the feed `<summary>` is used as the summary source rather than the short extracted body (AC-09-J)

userStories.title: Multi-profile tag merging on shared notes (profile union and Profile Relevance bullets)
userStories.description: As an Influx ingest pipeline running multiple profiles in one fire, I want `profile:*` tags and `## Profile Relevance` bullets union-merged on a single shared note so that one paper matching two profiles produces ONE note carrying both `profile:*` tags and two `## Profile Relevance` bullets (AC-M3-2, FR-NOTE-6).
acceptanceCriteria: When a single source item matches two profiles in one run, exactly ONE note is produced (AC-M3-2)
The shared note carries both `profile:<A>` and `profile:<B>` tags (set union, not replacement)
The shared note has two `## Profile Relevance` entries merged by profile name (FR-NOTE-6)
Disjoint matches still produce one note per profile, each tagged with only the matching `profile:*` tag (AC-M3-3)
Re-ingesting an already-tagged note preserves existing `profile:*` tags and `## Profile Relevance` entries for profiles that are not part of the current run (idempotency / no destructive overwrite)
Unit test `tests/unit/test_multi_profile_merge.py` covers: union semantics, entry merging by profile name, idempotency on re-ingest, and the disjoint-match case

Integration test `tests/integration/test_multi_profile_shared_source.py` drives a two-profile run against a fixture whose item matches both profiles and asserts one note with both tags and two `## Profile Relevance` entries merged by profile name

Negative test asserts that running one profile alone does NOT remove or rewrite an existing `profile:<other-profile>` tag or its `## Profile Relevance` entry on a shared note (e.g. running `web-tech` alone preserves an existing `profile:ai-robotics` tag and bullet)

userStories.title: Per-profile rejection authority preserved on the ingest path
userStories.description: As the ingest path, I want `influx:rejected:<profile>` to block re-adding `profile:<profile>` and to block refreshing that profile's `## Profile Relevance` entry, so that a per-profile rejection is preserved across reruns even when the source matches that profile again (AC-M3-6, AC-09-K, FR-NOTE-6).
acceptanceCriteria: When a note already has `influx:rejected:profile-a`, the ingest path MUST NOT re-add `profile:profile-a` even if the source matches that profile again (AC-M3-6)
When a note already has `influx:rejected:profile-a`, the ingest path MUST NOT refresh that profile's `## Profile Relevance` entry
If `profile-b` separately scores the same item ≥ `relevance` in the same run, `profile:profile-b` IS added on the same note, and the rejection on `profile-a` is preserved (AC-09-K)

In a multi-profile run, when an Influx-authored note carries `influx:rejected:<profile>`, that note's title appears in the next run's filter prompt under the `NEGATIVE EXAMPLES` block for that profile (AC-M3-5), exercised end-to-end via an integration test

Integration test `tests/integration/test_profile_rejection_authority.py` drives a two-profile run where one profile is rejected and the other is not, asserting the rejection-authority post-condition: `influx:rejected:<rejected-profile>` present, `profile:<rejected-profile>` absent, `profile:<accepted-profile>` present

userStories.title: Per-source fetch deduplication across profiles in a single scheduled fire
userStories.description: As the run coordinator, I want per-source fetches deduplicated across profiles within a single scheduled fire so that two profiles both subscribed to `cs.AI` fetch `cs.AI` exactly once, fanning the result out to all interested profiles (R-8 mitigation, AC-09-D).
acceptanceCriteria: When a scheduled fire executes multiple profiles concurrently, shared sources are not redundantly fetched across profiles within the run, and fetched items are fanned out to all profiles subscribed to them (R-8 mitigation)
Applies to both arXiv and RSS sources
Cross-profile parallelism remains allowed (Q-4); same-profile strict serial remains enforced by PRD 03's coordinator
Integration test asserts that with two profiles both subscribed to `cs.AI`, `cs.AI` is fetched exactly once for the run and its items are delivered to both profiles (AC-09-D)

userStories.title: Real /backfills HTTP handler with naive estimator and confirm gate
userStories.description: As the `/backfills` HTTP API, I want a real estimator and `confirm_required` gate so that backfills exceeding 1000 estimated items require explicit confirmation, replacing PRD 03's stub estimator (FR-BF-6, AC-M3-8, AC-09-E, AC-09-I).
acceptanceCriteria: Replace the PRD 03 stub `/backfills` handler in `src/influx/http_api.py` with a real implementation
The estimator computes `estimated_items = days × len(categories) × max_results_per_category` (Q-3)
The handler supports both backfill range forms required by FR-BF-1 (a number-of-days form and an explicit from/to date form)
When `estimated_items > 1000` and `confirm` is not set, the handler returns `reason="confirm_required"` with the `estimated_items` field populated (FR-BF-6, AC-M3-8)
When `estimated_items <= 1000`, the request is accepted without `confirm`
When `confirm=true` is supplied on a re-submission, the request is accepted regardless of estimated count

Unit tests in `tests/unit/test_backfill_estimator.py` cover: an estimate of 35 items accepted without confirm (AC-09-E); an estimate of 1500 items rejected with `reason="confirm_required"` and a populated `estimated_items` (AC-09-I); resubmission with `confirm=true` accepted (AC-09-I); estimator math with multiple categories

userStories.title: Backfill execution flow in CLI and run path with cache-lookup skip and arXiv pacing
userStories.description: As an operator running `python -m influx backfill`, I want the CLI to handle estimate responses, confirm prompting, and full execution against arXiv so that `backfill --profile X --days 7` completes without overlapping a scheduled run for the same profile (AC-M3-7, FR-BF-1, FR-BF-2, FR-BF-3).
acceptanceCriteria: Finalise the `backfill` subcommand in the project's CLI entrypoint: supports `--days N` or `--from YYYY-MM-DD --to YYYY-MM-DD` as a mutually exclusive range form (FR-BF-1)

On `reason="confirm_required"` from the API, the CLI surfaces `estimated_items` to the user and only submits `confirm=true` after explicit user acknowledgement — whether via a follow-up invocation with `--confirm` or an equivalent acknowledged retry flow (FR-BF-6)
Items already ingested (per `lithos_cache_lookup`) are skipped during the backfill run (FR-BF-2)
Backfill honours arXiv pacing / request spacing (FR-BF-3); the run respects the ~30s-per-day-of-backfill-per-profile budget
A backfill for profile `X` does not overlap with a scheduled run for the same profile; same-profile serialisation is enforced by PRD 03's coordinator (AC-M3-7)
Create `src/influx/backfill.py` housing the run flow (estimator integration, cache-lookup skip, pacing wiring)

Integration test `tests/integration/test_backfill_arxiv.py` drives `backfill --profile X --days 7` against a fake arXiv fixture and asserts: (1) the run completes, (2) already-ingested items are skipped (verified via the fake-Lithos cache-lookup recorder), (3) the run does not overlap with a concurrently scheduled run for the same profile

userStories.title: Propagate kind=backfill through task tagging, webhook gate, and repair-sweep gate
userStories.description: As the backfill pipeline, I want `kind="backfill"` propagated end-to-end so that backfills create `lithos_task_create` tasks tagged `influx:backfill` (not `influx:run`), never POST a webhook, and never call the repair sweep (AC-09-F, AC-09-G, AC-09-H, FR-BF-4, FR-BF-5, FR-NOT-4, FR-REP-2).
acceptanceCriteria: Along the run/task-creation path, `lithos_task_create` receives the tag `influx:backfill` when `kind="backfill"` and `influx:run` otherwise (FR-BF-5, FR-LCMA-5 backfill arm)
The backfill code path does not POST a webhook (FR-BF-4 / FR-NOT-4); the existing PRD 05 webhook gate is exercised end-to-end with `kind="backfill"` propagated from the API down (AC-09-G)
The backfill code path does not invoke `lithos_list(tags=["influx:repair-needed", ...])` (FR-REP-2); the existing PRD 06 repair-sweep gate is exercised end-to-end with `kind="backfill"` (AC-09-H)
Integration assertion in `tests/integration/test_backfill_arxiv.py` verifies the created Lithos task carries the tag `influx:backfill` and NOT `influx:run` (AC-09-F)
Integration assertion verifies that no webhook HTTP call was issued during the backfill (AC-09-G)
Integration assertion verifies that the repair sweep was not invoked during the backfill — i.e. no `lithos_list(tags=["influx:repair-needed", ...])` call recorded (AC-09-H)

Non-backfill regression test confirms that scheduled and manual runs continue to create `lithos_task_create` calls tagged `influx:run` rather than `influx:backfill`, so that the run-kind correctly selects between the two task tags (FR-BF-5)

definitionOfDone: All AC-M3-1 through AC-M3-8 (master PRD §7.3) are demonstrably exercised by tests
All AC-09-A through AC-09-K (PRD 09 §8 new-for-this-PRD) are demonstrably exercised by tests
No remaining stubs in the codebase except OTEL spans/metrics (PRD 10) and per-tag rejection-rate logging (PRD 10)
The `run_profile` hook left by PRD 03 is a single well-typed function that handles arXiv + RSS + scheduled + manual + backfill cases

`uv run pytest tests/unit/test_rss_fetcher.py tests/unit/test_archive_url_hash.py tests/unit/test_multi_profile_merge.py tests/unit/test_backfill_estimator.py tests/integration/test_rss_to_lithos.py tests/integration/test_multi_profile_shared_source.py tests/integration/test_backfill_arxiv.py tests/integration/test_profile_rejection_authority.py` all pass
Coverage on `src/influx/sources/rss.py`, `src/influx/backfill.py`, and the multi-profile orchestration code paths in `src/influx/service.py` is ≥ 80%
`uv run ruff check src tests` passes with zero violations on changed files
Backfill `kind="backfill"` is correctly propagated from the `/backfills` API down to task tagging, webhook gate, and repair-sweep gate
